commit.message:
fix: upgrade river 0.23.0 + numpy 2.2.3 for Python 3.13

river 0.21.2 has no pre-built musllinux wheels for cp313,
causing Docker build failure on Alpine. river 0.23.0 ships
with musllinux_1_2 wheels for both aarch64 and amd64.
Similarly numpy 1.26.4 doesn't support Python 3.13.

commit.message:
docs: add Track 5 — architecture customization (#218)

## Summary

4ward should handle lightly modified v1model definitions cleanly — no
hardcoded hacks. This is what SAI P4 needs: wider `PortId_t` (more than
2^9
ports) and string port names via `@p4runtime_translation`. The ecosystem
currently papers over this with hacks scattered across the stack. We can
do
better.

**Track 5** captures this as a first-class roadmap goal with three
milestones:

1. **Dynamic standard_metadata** — derive field widths from the IR, not
   hardcoded constants
2. **End-to-end `@p4runtime_translation`** — match fields + packet
metadata,
   not just action params
3. **SAI P4 on a modified v1model** — the proof it all works

Also promotes the roadmap to a prominent "Where we're headed" section in
the
README, right after "Why 4ward?" — with the north-star vision front and
center.

Subsumes Track 4C and 4E; renumbers old Track 5 (PSA) → Track 6.

## Test plan

- [ ] Docs-only change — verify rendered markdown reads well

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix templates not visible: switch from SQLAlchemy to pyodbc reads

Root cause: save_template used pyodbc, but list_templates and
get_template_by_id used SQLAlchemy engine. Connection pool read
consistency issue caused saved templates to be invisible.

Fix: All template reads now use pyodbc directly (same as writes).
Removed _get_engine(), fixed SQL injection in get_template_by_id
(f-string -> parameterized), errors now raised to caller instead
of silently swallowed by logger.warning.

commit.message:
Fix per-unit price comparison treating oz and fl oz as incomparable

The comparable_group method grouped items by normalized_unit, but "oz"
(weight) and "fl oz" (volume) were treated as different groups. This
caused the cheapest supplier highlight to fall back to raw case price
instead of per-unit price for products like juice where suppliers use
different unit conventions. Since 1 fl oz ≈ 1 oz for most food items,
merge them into one group for comparison purposes.

commit.message:
RIA-010: Persistence + RAID item analysis

Persistence: project name and 28 sub-criteria scores saved to localStorage
on every assess. Restored automatically on page load with dismiss notice.
Reset clears storage. Graceful handling of corrupt or missing state.

RAID item analysis: after assessment, user picks Risk / Issue / Assumption /
Dependency / Change, enters a description, and receives contextual analysis
from the Worker. Prompt includes project archetype, band, dimension scores,
weighted findings, and contradictions. Section locked until first assessment.

New modules: src/persistence.js, src/raid-item.js
New tests: 323 total (47 new)
New specs: specs/project-persistence.feature, specs/raid-item-analysis.feature

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Modularize prose_code.c into 12 modules + shared header

Split the monolithic 6,327-line prose_code.c into logically organized
modules following MODULARIZATION.md:

- prose_code.h: shared header with all types, macros, externs, prototypes
- buffer.c: arena allocator, gap buffer, line cache, wrap cache, undo stack
- theme.c: dark/light themes and DPI scaling
- spell.c: Windows ISpellChecker COM integration with static GUIDs
- syntax.c: C and Markdown tokenizers, keyword hash table
- document.c: document lifecycle, line/column math, gutter width
- editor.c: text editing ops, clipboard, undo/redo, cursor movement
- search.c: find/replace with match highlighting
- menu.c: menu definitions and action dispatch
- file_io.c: file I/O, atomic saves, autosave/crash recovery, tab management
- render.c: all GDI rendering (titlebar, tabs, editor, minimap, stats)
- wndproc.c: window procedure, input handling, bracket matching
- main.c: entry point, app icon, EditorState global

Also adds Makefile and GitHub Actions CI workflow for cross-compilation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Session 8: Entity Resolution — GLEIF LEI + OpenSanctions ingesters + screening API

- GLEIF LEI bulk ingester (A3): paginated fetch from api.gleif.org, upsert
  into entity_lei table (ON CONFLICT lei), weekly schedule (Sunday 3AM UTC)
- Sanctions ingester (A4): OpenSanctions consolidated (OFAC SDN, EU FSL,
  UN SC) via /search endpoint, FtM entity parsing, upsert into
  entity_sanctions table, daily schedule (4AM UTC)
- Entity resolution API routes: LEI search, sanctions search, sanctions
  screening (POST /screen with ILIKE matching), screening result review
  workflow (compliance role required), stats endpoint
- ORM models: EntityLei, EntitySanction, EntityScreeningResult
- Ingester registration in server.py lifespan startup
- 12/12 smoke tests passing

commit.message:
Correct false exponential moment theorem and prove Fatou bridge endpoint

Replace two mathematically false theorem statements:
1. gap_exponential_moment_geometric_bound (geometric decay impossible under
   probability measure — proved in test/proofideas_interaction_next_steps.lean)
2. gap_exp_interaction_uniform_bound with |V| (E[exp(p|V_κ|)] = ∞ for 4th
   Wiener chaos variables, since tails decay only as exp(-c√t))

The correct statement is gap_exp_neg_interaction_uniform_bound: the *negative*
exponential moments E[exp(-pV_κ)] are uniformly bounded in κ. This works
because :φ⁴: ≥ -6c² (right-skewed, bounded below), so exp(-V_κ) is controlled.
This is Nelson's bound (Simon Theorem V.14, Glimm-Jaffe Chapter 8.6).

Prove hasExpInteractionLp_of_analytic_inputs (sorry-free modulo its 2 inputs)
using the Fatou bridge from Part1Core, bypassing Part2/Part3 entirely.

Critical path reduced to 2 sorrys:
- gap_interactionCutoff_ae_convergence (a.e. UV convergence)
- gap_exp_neg_interaction_uniform_bound (Nelson's uniform bound)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: clear persisted customCenter when adding place so new place appears

getSearchCenter() returns customCenter||mapCenter||userLocation. A stale
customCenter persisted in localStorage was overriding the mapCenter update,
keeping the search center far from the new place. Clearing customCenter
(null) lets mapCenter=addedCoords become the effective search center.

commit.message:
Fix Connect disconnect-on-navigate — scope ViewModel to activity

Each module route was calling viewModel() scoped to its NavBackStackEntry,
creating separate ConnectViewModel instances. When navigating away from
connect_scan, the route-scoped ViewModel was cleared, calling disconnect()
and killing the connection that was just established.

Fix: scope all ConnectViewModel instances to the activity so all connect
routes share a single instance, matching ShellNavGraph behavior.

- 302 tests, 16 files, all passing

commit.message:
Only mark tickers inactive on full syncs, not 5d/recent syncs

During 5d/recent mode downloads, a "ticker_skipped" event just means
Tiingo has no recent EOD data for that ticker (common for lower-volume
ETFs with delayed data). Permanently marking them inactive caused their
parquet files to stop updating, leading to stale historical data.

Now markTickerInactive is only called during full mode syncs, where
"no data at all" is genuine evidence that a ticker is delisted.
